title: Complex number conjugates
description: Given a complex number, find its conjugate or plot it in the complex plane.
topic_path: Complex conjugates and dividing complex numbers

hints:
###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{6}i\maroonD{-7}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{-7}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{6}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=-\tealD{6}i\maroonD{-7}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/01816afbd345449b58182671d76a0868e769166b)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-6i-7$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{5}i+\maroonD{5}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{5}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{5}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\tealD{-5}i+\maroonD{5}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a16689e37594e8b41583aaf4e5e70d3afd718282)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-5i+5$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{-4}i\maroonD{-6}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{-6}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{-4}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\tealD{4}i\maroonD{-6}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27185c7d3515f77c2c757d82f3c0a7adeb41b6dc)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=4i-6$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{-7}i+\maroonD{6}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{6}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{-7}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\tealD{7}i+\maroonD{6}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a1fcf5a58988714a463de866d4a40cc67a662920)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=7i+6$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{-2}i+\maroonD{7}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{7}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{-2}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\tealD{2}i+\maroonD{7}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ecfb6ec447087c8295d34f8a553d7bd7ab8732f5)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=2i+7$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{1}i+\maroonD{4}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{4}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{1}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=-\tealD{1}i+\maroonD{4}=-i+\maroonD{4}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/35bef83d66d049e3b2ae888f55944b957648c52f)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-i+4$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{3}i+\maroonD{8}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{8}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{3}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=-\tealD{3}i+\maroonD{8}$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/424ea3732e85288ba4c4636a3fa1e5c07955c535)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-3i+8$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{8}i$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{0}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{8}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=-\tealD{8}i$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c83e9dc64daeb3f2110ddd4f5e80053866176144)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-8i$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$.

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\tealD{-4}i+\maroonD{4}$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{4}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{-4}$.

$\bar{z_1}=4i+4$

###Background

###Background

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\maroonD{-1}+\tealD{5}i$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{-1}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{5}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\maroonD{-1}-\tealD{5}i$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e3410d48f1de4af8251de59115ec183ddefed208)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-1-5i$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\maroonD{6}+\tealD{1}i$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{6}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{1}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\maroonD{6}-\tealD{1}i=\maroonD{6}-i$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1f050664ae238abab1f6f1b8171d43311f3748b)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=6-i$

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

We see that $z_1=\maroonD{-7}+\tealD{3}i$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{-7}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{3}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\maroonD{-7}-\tealD{3}i$. This is shown in the graph below.

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=-7-3i$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\maroonD{2}+\tealD{2}i$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{2}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{2}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\maroonD{2}-\tealD{2}i$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6143f1a238f0f1b5556f99443256a6fcab0e8f2e)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=2-2i$

###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Finding the complex conjugate

We see that $z_1=\maroonD{8}+\tealD{1}i$ is of the form $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$, where $\maroonD{a}=\maroonD{8}$ and  $\tealD{b}=\tealD{1}$. 

Therefore, by reversing the sign of its imaginary part, the complex conjugate of $z_1$ is given by $\bar{z_1}=\maroonD{8}-\tealD{1}i=\maroonD{8}-i$. This is shown in the graph below. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2162abd2332813901926ce78a25c33f78c04cd6c)

###Summary 

$\bar{z_1}=8-i$

assessment_items.question_content:
**A complex number $z_1$ is plotted below. Plot its complex conjugate, $\bar{z_1}$.** 

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints:
###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Plotting the Complex Conjugate

The complex conjugate of $z_1$ is just the reflection of $z_1$ across the real axis. This is plotted below. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/10ea9b781bdbbb6c3ffb29e60655785470dda416)

###Summary


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0317aae9be1ef5b15aeef88483f65b19c244d940)

assessment_items.question_content:
**A complex number $z_1$ is plotted below. Plot its complex conjugate, $\bar{z_1}$.** 

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints:
###Background 

The complex conjugate of a complex number $z=\maroonD{a}+\tealD{b}i$ is given by $\bar{z}=\maroonD{a}-\tealD{b}i$. 

This is equal to the original complex number, but with the sign of the imaginary part reversed. On the complex plane, it is the reflection of the original point across the real axis. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Plotting the Complex Conjugate

The complex conjugate of $z_1$ is just the reflection of $z_1$ across the real axis. This is plotted below. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/beced92bbe965dd200416c09924867e58afcd43c)

###Summary


![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/beced92bbe965dd200416c09924867e58afcd43c)
